era: 1988-1993 full run. Post-Hardcore. E=8 -- energy escalation from E=7 at first appearance. Maximum melodic ambition, rhythmic complexity, emotional directness. Boise to Seattle.

theory_nodes.concept: Energy escalation E=7 to E=8: documented artistic development. Just Kidding (1993) is the band at maximum. Rare escalation across engine appearances.
theory_nodes.description: The return escalates energy because the band developed. Just Kidding is more extended and harmonically developed than any previous Treepeople recording.

theory_nodes.concept: C/Z Records double-return 297-298: Skin Yard and Treepeople return in adjacent degrees, closing the label's four-degree cluster in the engine (263, 264, 297, 298).
theory_nodes.description: C/Z Records fully documented across four degrees. The pre-Sub Pop heavy underground infrastructure is now complete in the engine.

theory_nodes.concept: Treepeople as Built to Spill laboratory: guitar vocabulary, extended song structures, rhythmic complexity all developed in Treepeople and carried directly into Built to Spill.
theory_nodes.description: Two Treepeople entries (263, 298) provide the full pre-Built foundation. Future Built to Spill entry is contextualized.

cross_references.relationship: First appearance E=7. Boise-Seattle pipeline. Doug Martsch. C/Z. Pre-Built context.
C/Z double-return. Adjacent degrees close the C/Z cluster.
C/Z labelmate. First appearances adjacent at 263-264; returns adjacent at 297-298. Symmetrical label documentation.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=8 + E=8 = E=16. Maximum pairing in the return cluster. Treepeople's E=8 post-hardcore meets the Goshawk's sustained ka-ka-ka assault. Both unrelenting at maximum effort.
